question: How do I contact a human at Al-Fardan?
answer: Four options: (1) Email support@alfardanq9.com — typical response under 2 UAE business hours. (2) In-app support ticket under Settings → Support — goes directly to our desk team. (3) Phone +971-4-xxx-xxxx during UAE business hours (Sun-Thu 9am-6pm GST). (4) For existing institutional clients, your dedicated relationship manager. Urgent account security issues → always phone first.

question: What are your business hours?
answer: UAE business hours: Sunday to Thursday, 9am to 6pm GST (Gulf Standard Time, UTC+4). Friday and Saturday the desk is closed but we monitor urgent security issues 24/7. For global/institutional clients on other timezones we offer coverage windows — ask your relationship manager.

question: What languages do you support?
answer: English and Arabic across the full product — portal, emails, support, documentation. Support team has native Arabic speakers for Emirati and GCC clients. Product UI can be toggled between English and Arabic in Settings → Preferences. Additional languages on roadmap: French and Hindi (for significant UAE resident populations) in 2026.

question: Can I have multiple accounts with Al-Fardan?
answer: Individuals: one personal account per person. For multiple legal entities (your personal account + a family office SPV + a corporate account), each entity gets its own separate account with its own KYC. This keeps books clean and compliance proper. Sub-users (authorised signatories, finance staff) can be added to corporate accounts under the parent account — not separate accounts.

question: Do you have an iOS or Android app?
answer: Not yet — the portal is fully responsive and works in any mobile browser (Safari iOS, Chrome Android). Dedicated native iOS and Android apps are on the 2026 roadmap (Q2-Q3). For a better mobile experience: bookmark the portal to your home screen (iOS: Share → Add to Home Screen; Android: browser menu → Add to Home Screen).

question: Are my funds insured?
answer: Yes. All custody is covered under our Lloyd's of London insurance policy (policy SY-2025-49881) with coverage in the hundreds of millions USD. The policy covers cold-storage-custody loss, theft from Fireblocks infrastructure, and most insider-fraud scenarios. It does NOT cover: (1) individual-client account takeover where the attacker had your password + 2FA (our security features are designed to prevent this), or (2) loss from your end — e.g. you sending crypto to a scammer. Custody-layer loss = covered.

question: What happens if Al-Fardan goes bankrupt?
answer: All client assets are held in dedicated Fireblocks MPC vaults, legally segregated from Al-Fardan's operational assets. Even in a bankruptcy scenario, your crypto doesn't become part of the bankruptcy estate — it's already yours, held in trust. The bankruptcy trustee's role would be to transfer the Fireblocks policy-signing rights to a successor custodian, after which you'd get access back normally. Segregation + Fireblocks structure is specifically designed to prevent the FTX-style commingled-funds catastrophe.

question: Can I bring my own external wallet?
answer: Yes — deposits and withdrawals work with any external wallet you control. Send from your Ledger/Trezor/MetaMask to our deposit address; withdraw from us back to any address of yours. We don't lock you into an Al-Fardan-only ecosystem. Many clients use us as the cold-treasury layer and keep a small hot operational wallet self-custodied for day-to-day.

question: Who can use Al-Fardan — which countries?
answer: Primary market: UAE residents (Emirati, expat, family-office). We also service institutional clients globally subject to KYC passing + your jurisdiction not being on the UN/OFAC sanctions list. We do NOT onboard clients from US, Canada, or OFAC-sanctioned countries. If your jurisdiction isn't obviously blocked, start the signup — our KYC team will confirm on review.

question: What's your minimum deposit to open an account?
answer: No minimum for opening a custody account or completing KYC. You can open and verify with zero deposit. Each service has its own activity minimum: staking 1 ETH or 1 SOL, lending $25K loan minimum, OTC $100K ticket minimum. You can open the account, explore, and deposit only when ready to use a service.

question: Do you have a referral program?
answer: Yes — existing clients get a referral link in Settings → Referrals. If someone signs up via your link and opens an active account (deposits ≥$10K in any service within 60 days), you get 5 bps annual fee credit for their lifetime on the platform. No limit on how many referrals. Ask your relationship manager for details for institutional referrals.

question: Can I trade 24/7?
answer: Custody and withdrawals: 24/7 with auto-processing. Staking: 24/7 protocol-level. OTC: UAE business hours for standard quotes (Sun-Thu 9am-6pm); 24/7 with advance arrangement for VIP clients. Lending: applications 24/7, admin review during business hours. Loan repayments: any time but bank-wire settlement follows UAE banking hours.

question: Do you offer margin or leverage?
answer: No — we don't offer margin trading or leveraged products. Our lending product is technically a form of leverage (you borrow against your crypto), but it's long-term structured credit, not a retail margin position. No auto-liquidation on price dips, no forced buy-ins, no perpetual futures. This is a deliberate choice to avoid the retail margin-call blowups that took out FTX and similar venues.

question: Do you have an API for institutional integration?
answer: Yes — REST API for institutional clients with a $1M+ AUM account. Covers: balances, transaction history, deposit address generation, withdrawal submission (rate-limited + whitelisted), OTC quote requests. Authentication via HMAC-SHA256 with scoped API keys (read-only, trade, or withdraw permissions separately). Full docs delivered on VIP onboarding. Ask your relationship manager.

question: Can I close my account?
answer: Yes. Withdraw all assets to your external wallet(s), repay any active loans, let any staking positions unbond. Once balances are zero, email support@alfardanq9.com requesting account closure. We retain KYC records for 7 years per UAE AML rules but the account itself is deactivated within 3 business days. No exit fee.

question: Why should I use your platform instead of a regular exchange?
answer: Regular retail exchange: great for small fast trades, limited insurance, commingled user funds, high spreads on large tickets, regulatory uncertainty in many jurisdictions. Al-Fardan institutional custody: segregated Fireblocks vaults, Lloyd's-insured, VARA-regulated, tight OTC spreads, no-lockup staking, long-term asset-backed credit, dedicated human support. Best use of Al-Fardan: cold treasury (custody + optional staking), large-ticket trades (OTC), and crypto-collateralised credit. For $100/month retail trades, a retail exchange is fine; for institutional balance sheets, institutional custody is the right structure.

question: Are you a bank?
answer: No. We're a regulated digital-asset custody + services provider under VARA (Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority) in the UAE. We don't take fiat deposits as a bank would, don't offer fiat savings accounts, and aren't covered by UAE deposit-insurance schemes. We work alongside banks: you wire fiat from your bank to us for OTC/loan settlement, or from us to your bank for liquidation proceeds.

question: How do I get an institutional account?
answer: Corporate/family-office/fund clients: start with our institutional onboarding form (contact support to request). We'll schedule a call with your relationship manager to understand your needs, then walk through KYC: certificate of incorporation, UBO declaration, board resolution, bank references, and individual KYC for authorised signatories. Typical end-to-end: 3-5 UAE business days. Institutional clients get: higher daily limits, tighter OTC spreads, OTC coverage outside UAE hours, API access, custom reporting.

question: What's your privacy policy?
answer: Summary: We collect what's required for KYC/AML (name, ID, address, phone, email), plus what's needed to operate the service (transaction history, device used for login). We don't sell data to third parties. We share data only with: (1) regulators on legitimate legal request, (2) our auditor during annual audits, (3) Fireblocks for custody operations, (4) our bank for fiat settlements. Full policy in Settings → Legal or at alfardanq9.com/privacy.

question: Do you report to tax authorities?
answer: UAE has no personal income or capital gains tax on crypto, so no automatic reporting to UAE tax authorities. For non-UAE residents we comply with FATCA (US persons) and CRS (OECD members) where legally required — for US/EU residents we may report account balances to your home tax authority per treaty obligations. We provide annual tax statements for your own accountant to use regardless.
